name: Vimeo API
what_it_does: Video hosting and sharing platform with an API for uploading, managing, and embedding professional video content with privacy controls.
use_cases: Uploading and organizing video libraries programmatically
Managing video metadata, thumbnails, and captions from agents
Retrieving video analytics and engagement stats
not_for: Live streaming at scale (Mux or Twitch are better)
Free or low-budget projects needing large storage
best_when: You need reliable video hosting with privacy controls, a clean player, and API access for managing a video library.
avoid_when: You need live streaming, high-throughput video encoding, or very detailed analytics.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 for user-authenticated requests. Personal access tokens (app tokens) for server-side access. Scopes control read/write/delete permissions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 500MB/week upload, 5GB total storage, basic features
pricing.notes: API access requires at least a paid plan for production use. Free tier is severely limited. OAuth app creation available on all tiers.

performance.rate_limits: 1000 API requests per minute per access token

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error messages include error code and developer message. Docs are somewhat dated and not always accurate for newer endpoints.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency key support. Duplicate uploads create duplicate videos.
known_agent_gotchas: Video uploads use a tus-based resumable protocol - not a simple POST, requires specialized upload logic
OAuth token scopes must be explicitly requested and approved by user flow
Rate limits are per access token, not per app - agents with many users can exceed limits
Transcoding is async - agents must poll video status or use webhooks before embedding
Some API endpoints require specific paid plan features - errors are not always clear about this
